description:
Bad look for a mutation-testing tool: `mix test --cover` produces 3 failures and an Enum.EmptyError crash in the coverage reporter.

Root cause: two assertions assume :cover_server is NOT running, which cover itself violates:
- test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_test.exs:374 — `refute Process.whereis(:cover_server)`
- test/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver_property_test.exs:164 — same

Fix: guard or skip those assertions when running under cover (e.g. detect cover and skip, or assert the no-side-effect invariant a different way). Also investigate the Enum.EmptyError in report generation (:cover compiled zero project modules).

Acceptance: `mix test --cover` runs clean and prints a coverage number.

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED (recorded by orchestrator on behalf of impl worker — worker committed but its subagent permission mode blocked bw writes).

Commit: d99e1c4 on mutagen-hcs.2/fix-cover. Diff is test-only (7 files), base 196bb8c.

Verification (worker-run):
- mix test --cover: 623 tests, 0 failures (36 excluded) | Coverage: 78.96%
- mix test (normal): 640 tests, 0 failures (19 excluded)
- Base reproduced all 3 original failures + Enum.EmptyError; all gone now.

Both original failure modes resolved: the two cover-server `refute Process.whereis(:cover_server)` assertions (scope_resolver_test.exs:374, scope_resolver_property_test.exs:164) and the Enum.EmptyError crash in the coverage reporter.

Approach: introduced a :cover_lifecycle test tag + test/support/cover_guard.exs, excluded only under --cover (detected via Process.whereis(:cover_server)). Each "extra" file beyond the two named was verified load-bearing by reverting in isolation:
- test_helper.exs: applies :cover_lifecycle exclusion under --cover (the spine).
- coverage_runner_test.exs / supervision_test.exs: tests that drive cover-conflicting paths; tagged :cover_lifecycle.
- mutation_runner_test.exs + cover_guard.exs: guards a defensive on_exit :cover.stop/0 that was discarding harness instrumentation (the EmptyError source).

CALLOUT for verify: `mix test --cover` exits non-zero (code 3) due to Mix's DEFAULT 90% coverage threshold (78.96% < 90%) — NOT a test failure or crash, and not configured in mix.exs (no test_coverage block). Ticket acceptance is "runs clean (0 failures) + prints a coverage number" — met. Raising coverage to 90% is a separate ticket.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 033814be-550f-48f5-ab4a-ac141d9e94a3
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/033814be-550f-48f5-ab4a-ac141d9e94a3.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 7 (all under test/** — yes). coverage_runner_test.exs, mutation_runner_test.exs, scope_resolver_property_test.exs, scope_resolver_test.exs, supervision_test.exs, test/support/cover_guard.exs (new), test/test_helper.exs. No lib/** or other product change.

Semantic checks:
- mix test --cover: test failures = 0 (3 of 4 full-suite runs), Enum.EmptyError crash present = no (0/4 runs), coverage printed = yes (78.96% Total), exit code = 3 (cause: threshold — tail reads "Coverage test failed, threshold not met: Coverage 78.96% / Threshold 90.00%"; stock Mix default, no test_coverage block in mix.exs).
- mix test (normal): 640 tests, 0 failures (19 excluded), exit 0.
- Base 196bb8c reproduced (read-only, main worktree): scope_resolver tests fail 2/2 under --cover on the two `refute Process.whereis(:cover_server)` assertions. EmptyError path also pre-existing per ticket. Fix removes both.
- Fix soundness (real resolution vs masking): SOUND.
  * The two named assertions are rewritten from bare `refute Process.whereis(:cover_server)` to before/after EQUALITY (`assert Process.whereis(:cover_server) == cover_server_before` and same for File.exists?("cover")). This is a strictly MORE correct statement of the real invariant — "the resolver does not start cover or create cover/" — and degrades to the original refute in normal mode (before == nil). Not a mask; it fixes a wrong assertion that conflated "harness owns cover" with "resolver started cover".
  * :cover_lifecycle exclusion is legitimate. Tagged tests manipulate the BEAM-wide :cover_server singleton (register sentinels, call :cover.stop, or drive CoverageRunner.run/1 which CORRECTLY refuses with :cover_already_running when cover is already registered). Under --cover the harness owns cover, so these cannot run as written — excluding ONLY under --cover is correct; they still run on every normal `mix test` (verified: the two key assertion tests execute, 2 tests/0 failures in normal mode; normal run excludes 19 vs --cover excludes 36, delta = the cover_lifecycle set). Not sweeping a product bug under the rug — CoverageRunner's refusal is the documented, desired behavior.
  * cover_guard.exs running_under_cover? uses a >1 module-count threshold so a test's own single-module cover session is not mistaken for the harness — sound. The mutation_runner on_exit guard skips the defensive :cover.stop only under harness cover, which is exactly the EmptyError source (stopping cover discarded harness instrumentation -> 0 modules -> reporter crash).

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only):
- One non-deterministic --cover failure observed in 1 of 4 full-suite runs: mutation_runner_test.exs:1181 ("r11 ... byte-identical before/after"), flagging a /tmp entry `mutagen_ex_selector_3587`. That entry originates from test_selector_test.exs:35 (System tmp dir, `mutagen_ex_selector_#{unique_integer}`), NOT from the runner under test. r11 snapshots /tmp and attributes any `mutagen_ex_`-prefixed entry to the runner, so a concurrent/lingering test_selector tmp dir inside the snapshot window is misattributed. This is PRE-EXISTING flaky cross-test /tmp contamination in the r11 assertion — the diff does not touch r11 (line 1181), DiskSnapshot, or test_selector_test; the only mutation_runner_test edits are the cover_guard require (line 33) and the on_exit guard (lines 55-72). In isolation r11 passes; 3 of 4 full runs were clean. Recommend a follow-up ticket to tighten r11's tmp attribution (e.g. snapshot only tmp entries created by THIS test's pid/window, or scope the prefix to the runner's own tmp namespace).

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: 0eb050fa-440a-426e-9f51-bfd4d1ee50de
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/0eb050fa-440a-426e-9f51-bfd4d1ee50de.jsonl

Reasoning:

FLAKE (load-bearing): r11 did NOT flake in 28 runs on the stage branch (22x `mix test --cover` + 6x normal). The diff cannot increase the flake rate, and the verifier's pre-existing/unrelated judgment holds — with one correction to its root cause. r11's tmp check (mutation_runner_test.exs:1181) flags any /tmp entry with prefix `mutagen_ex` (DiskSnapshot.mutagen_attributable_tmp default "mutagen_ex"). That prefix is broad: it catches `mutagen_ex_selector_*` (test_selector_test.exs:34) AND many other async siblings (mutagen_ex_lane_, mutagen_ex_c1_, mutagen_ex_archives_test_, plus determinism/json_reporter/etc.). So this is a pre-existing weakness in r11's attribution, not specific to test_selector. Critically, the two `:cover_lifecycle`-excluded modules (coverage_runner_test, supervision_test) create ZERO `mutagen_ex_`-prefixed tmp dirs, and the exclusion drops only async:false modules — it cannot make async test_selector overlap the sync r11 (ExUnit keeps async/sync phases disjoint), and it touches neither r11, DiskSnapshot, nor any tmp-creating module. No mechanism by which this diff surfaces/worsens the flake.

SOUNDNESS: Real resolution, not a mask. The two named assertions (scope_resolver_test.exs:374, scope_resolver_property_test.exs:164) are rewritten from `refute Process.whereis(:cover_server)` to before/after EQUALITY — strictly the correct invariant ("the resolver causes no cover side effect"), degrades to the old refute in normal mode (before==nil), and is not vacuous (resolver starting cover or creating cover/ still fails it). These tests are NOT cover_lifecycle-tagged: verified they run+pass in both modes (normal: 2 tests/0 fail; --cover: 1 test/0 fail). The :cover_lifecycle exclusion only drops tests that genuinely cannot run under harness-owned cover (CoverageRunner.run/1 correctly refuses :cover_already_running; sentinel-registration tests would evict the harness). cover_guard.exs's >1 module-count threshold correctly distinguishes a test's single-module session from the harness. Scope: 0 lib/** changes, all 7 files under test/** (confirmed against real diff). exit=3 is threshold-only (78.96% < default 90%), out of scope as stated.

FOLLOW-UP (do NOT make .2 own it): The r11 flake is real-but-pre-existing — recommend a follow-up ticket to tighten r11's tmp attribution (scope the prefix to the runner's own namespace, or snapshot only entries created within this test's pid/window), since the current `mutagen_ex` prefix misattributes any async sibling's scratch dir.

APPROVED (orchestrator). VERIFY: PASS + AUDIT: APPROVE (auditor ran 28 trials, no flake on stage; proved the :cover_lifecycle exclusion cannot worsen the pre-existing r11 /tmp-attribution flake; confirmed the assertion rewrite is a stricter invariant, not a mask). Rebased onto epic/hcs (test_helper.exs union resolved by rebase worker), then merged --ff-only. Epic gates green: smoke 642/0, mix test --cover 0 failures + 79.18% printed (exit 3 = 90% threshold only), format clean, deps.unlock clean. Closing.

title: Fix mix test --cover (3 failures + crash)
